tool.name: create_roe
tool.description:
Create the formal Rules of Engagement document for this scan.
Call this FIRST, before any recon or exploitation activity, so every downstream decision can be validated against a written scope. Produces two files in the sandbox: `/workspace/engagement/<slug>/roe.md` (human-readable) and `roe.json` (machine-readable digest).
The RoE is the authoritative authorization statement from the client. If any in-flight operation would exceed the scope defined here, STOP and ask for clarification.

parameter.name: engagement_name
parameter.description: Short name for this engagement (e.g., "acme_q2_webapp_audit"). Used as the slug for the output directory.

parameter.name: client_name
parameter.description: Legal or display name of the client organization.

parameter.name: authorized_by
parameter.description: Name and title of the person at the client who authorized the engagement.

parameter.name: in_scope
parameter.description: List of in-scope targets: domains, IPs, CIDR ranges, application paths, API endpoints. Be specific.

parameter.name: out_of_scope
parameter.description: Explicitly out-of-scope systems. Third-party services, production databases, customer data stores.

parameter.name: allowed_techniques
parameter.description: Optional list of allowed techniques (e.g., "SQL injection testing", "XSS reflection"). Leave empty to allow all standard pentest techniques except forbidden_techniques.

parameter.name: forbidden_techniques
parameter.description: Techniques the client has explicitly forbidden (e.g., "DoS testing", "Credential stuffing with real user accounts").

parameter.name: testing_window_start
parameter.description: ISO timestamp when testing may begin (e.g., "2025-05-01T09:00:00Z").

parameter.name: testing_window_end
parameter.description: ISO timestamp when testing must stop.

parameter.name: notification_channel
parameter.description: Primary comms channel for status updates (Slack channel, email list, Signal group).

parameter.name: emergency_contact
parameter.description: 24/7 contact for urgent issues (name, phone, email).

parameter.name: destructive_actions_allowed
parameter.description: Whether destructive actions (data deletion, service disruption) are permitted. Default false.

parameter.name: data_exfiltration_allowed
parameter.description: Whether actual data exfiltration is permitted. Default false — evidence via screenshots and hashes only.

parameter.name: social_engineering_allowed
parameter.description: Whether social engineering (phishing, vishing) is permitted. Default false.

parameter.name: production_allowed
parameter.description: Whether production systems are in scope. Default true (operate with care).

parameter.name: third_party_infrastructure_notice
parameter.description: Notice about any third-party services the target depends on and how to handle their discovery.

returns.description: Success flag, markdown_path, json_path, engagement_slug, and digest dict.

tool.name: create_conops
tool.description:
Create the Concept of Operations — mission, objectives, adversary model, starting posture.
Call this after create_roe. The ConOps answers "what are we simulating, why, and how" and is used by the root agent to stay aligned with the engagement's strategic goals. Writes to `/workspace/engagement/<slug>/conops.md`.

parameter.name: engagement_name
parameter.description: Same name used in create_roe (for slug consistency).

parameter.name: mission_statement
parameter.description: One-paragraph mission statement — why this engagement exists.

parameter.name: primary_objectives
parameter.description: Ordered list of concrete objectives (e.g., "Demonstrate external-to-internal compromise path", "Identify OWASP Top 10 exposures in the customer portal").

parameter.name: success_criteria
parameter.description: What defines a successful engagement — deliverables, findings quality bar, evidence standards.

parameter.name: threat_actor_profile
parameter.description: Name of a threat actor being emulated (apt29, apt28, lazarus, fin7, scattered_spider) or free-form description. Leave empty for generic attacker.

parameter.name: testing_approach
parameter.description: Black-box, grey-box, or white-box; authenticated or unauthenticated.

parameter.name: assumed_starting_point
parameter.description: Where the attacker begins: "external" (internet), "internal_lan", "compromised_user", "supply_chain", etc.

parameter.name: kill_chain_focus
parameter.description: Kill chain phases to prioritize: initial_access, execution, persistence, privilege_escalation, etc. Omit to cover all.

returns.description: Success flag, path to conops.md, engagement_slug.

tool.name: create_opplan
tool.description:
Create the Operations Plan — phased execution playbook with agents, skills, techniques per phase.
Call this after create_conops. The OPPLAN is the step-by-step plan the root agent executes and that sub-agents reference when starting work. Writes to `/workspace/engagement/<slug>/opplan.md` and a JSON digest.

parameter.name: engagement_name
parameter.description: Same name used in create_roe.

parameter.name: phases
parameter.description: Ordered list of phase dicts. Each phase should include: name, objective, agents, skills, techniques, duration, deliverables. See examples for structure.

parameter.name: estimated_duration
parameter.description: Total estimated duration (e.g., "4 hours", "2 days").

parameter.name: required_tools
parameter.description: Tools the plan depends on (e.g., ["nmap", "sliver", "msfconsole", "sqlmap"]).

parameter.name: risk_considerations
parameter.description: Engagement-specific risks to mitigate (e.g., "Production database — read-only queries only", "Stable business hours — avoid 9am-5pm testing").

parameter.name: rollback_procedures
parameter.description: How to clean up created artifacts, test accounts, persistence, uploaded files at end of engagement.

tool.name: create_deconfliction_plan
tool.description:
Create the Deconfliction Plan — how to coordinate with the blue team and avoid mistaking test activity for real incidents.
Call this after create_opplan if the engagement involves an active blue team, SOC, or incident response team. Defines IOC notification, artifact tagging, and pause conditions. Writes to `/workspace/engagement/<slug>/deconfliction.md`.

parameter.name: engagement_name
parameter.description: Same name used in create_roe.

parameter.name: concurrent_operations
parameter.description: Any other operations or engagements the client has in parallel that might collide.

parameter.name: blue_team_contacts
parameter.description: Blue team / SOC points of contact for deconfliction.

parameter.name: ioc_notification_plan
parameter.description: How test IOCs are shared with defenders in advance so they can distinguish real from simulated.

parameter.name: test_artifact_tagging
parameter.description: Convention for marking every test artifact (files, accounts, payloads) so they are identifiable and cleanable.

parameter.name: pause_conditions
parameter.description: Conditions that require immediate pause and client notification. Defaults to sensible list.

returns.description: Success flag, path to deconfliction.md, engagement_slug.

tool.name: get_engagement_package
tool.description:
Read the full engagement package (RoE, ConOps, OPPLAN, Deconfliction Plan) back into the current agent's context.
Use this at the start of each phase to re-verify scope, or when a sub-agent needs to see the authoritative boundaries before taking action.

parameter.name: engagement_slug
parameter.description: The slug of the engagement (same as engagement_name passed to create_roe, with special chars replaced).

returns.description: Full package dict with roe_md, roe_json, conops_md, opplan_md, opplan_json, deconfliction_md. Any missing files are listed in the `missing` field.
